design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate underground mines in zero-gravity 6DOF flight, locate and destroy reactors, escape before self-destruct, engage hostile robots
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure (reactor countdown) combined with spatial disorientation in three-dimensional environments; navigation complexity vs. combat threat
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Piloting a fighter spacecraft through claustrophobic alien mines with unrestricted movement in all axes; mastery of 3D space combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commits fully to six-degrees-of-freedom gameplay mechanics that differentiate it fundamentally from standard FPS conventions of the era. Rejects on-rails perspective; player has complete 3D agency. The design prioritizes spatial puzzle-solving and disorientation as core tension rather than simplifying into cardinal directions

difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based; secret levels cannot be saved and require teleporting back to regular levels to save progress
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multiple difficulty modes available; player can manage risk via Guide-Bot navigation assist, automap use, flares/headlight for visibility, and optional hostage rescues for bonus points

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon system expansion via power-ups; primary weapons (pulse lasers, plasma bolts) and secondary weapons scattered throughout levels; energy management as resource constraint
progression.unlock_mechanism: Key-based door system (blue, yellow, red keys); colored doors gate access to new areas. Secret levels hidden behind locked doors with teleporters; 6 secret levels out of 30 total (20%) are optional
replayability_hooks: Secret levels with high power-up density can be revisited once discovered
Optional hostage rescues for bonus points encourage thoroughness
Multiplayer mode separate from single-player campaign

world_building_techniques: Star system theming (six differently themed star systems create environmental variety)
Thematic room design within mines (corridors, chambers with contextual layout)
Robot variety and behavior suggests different enemy factions/purposes

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters replace reactor destruction on every fourth level, establishing signature milestone encounters within linear progression

faction_indicators: Multiple robot types across different star systems suggest organized opposition
Robot generators as infrastructure implies coordinated occupation of mines
Hostage presence indicates captured human forces
danger_signals: Infected robots attempting to destroy player ship
Reactor self-destruct mechanism as doomsday device
Boss robot encounters at regular intervals
environmental_storytelling: Mine structure and layout conveys industrial occupation
Colored key/door systems imply security architecture and restricted zones
Control panels that trigger events (doors, walls, force fields) suggest active facility management
Energy stations indicate infrastructure supporting extended operations
